message:
fix(search): tombstone stale main-index entries on WAL append to prevent score leakage

When a document was re-indexed (appended to the WAL), the old main-index
entry could still appear in search results if its score exceeded the WAL
entry's score. The previous dedup in resolve_sorted_entries only suppressed
main-index entries whose WAL counterpart was already a top-K winner.

Fix in two layers: on append, immediately tombstone the matching main-index
record so the heap scanner never considers it. Replace the winners-only
wal_doc_ids set with a full scan of wal_entries when filtering main-index
results. Adds regression test stale_main_entry_shadowed_by_wal. Fixes
flaky proptest ordering assertion and corrects rank vector direction in
search_top_k_with_params tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
